text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY -- FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Re-biopsy Results Review and Treatment Planning

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Ms. Margaret Yuen is a 64-year-old woman with Stage IV (M1b) EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, now presenting for oncology follow-up after completion of stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) to three intracranial metastases and to review results of repeat tumor tissue biopsy with next-generation sequencing. The purpose of today's visit is multifold: to assess post-SRS neurological recovery, to review comprehensive re-profiling NGS data, and to finalize a second-line systemic therapy plan.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Yuen was originally diagnosed with Stage IIIB (T3N3M0) right upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma harboring an EGFR L858R point mutation (exon 21, allele frequency 38%) approximately 14 months ago. She initiated osi (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily as first-line therapy given her actionable EGFR mutation. She achieved a robust partial response, with her primary right upper lobe mass shrinking from 5.8 cm to approximately 1.8 cm and normalization of mediastinal and supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. She had no evidence of systemic progression for 12 months on Tagrisso.

Approximately one month prior to her last visit, she developed new-onset bifrontal headaches (worse in the morning) and mild word-finding difficulties over two to three weeks. Urgent MRI brain at that time revealed three new enhancing intracranial lesions: a 12 mm left frontal lesion with surrounding vasogenic edema, an 8 mm right cerebellar lesion, and a 6 mm left parietal lesion. Concurrent CT chest demonstrated growth of the right upper lobe mass from 1.8 cm to 2.7 cm and a new right paratracheal node measuring 1.1 cm, confirming concurrent systemic progression. Liquid biopsy (Guardant360) obtained at that time detected an EGFR T790M resistance mutation at a low allele frequency (0.6%), along with persistence of EGFR L858R (1.2%) and TP53 R248W (1.1%). osi was discontinued at that visit. She was started on dexamethasone 4 mg twice daily for cerebral edema and was urgently referred to radiation oncology.

Approximately six weeks after progression was identified, she underwent Gamma Knife SRS to all three intracranial metastases: 20 Gy to the left frontal lesion, 20 Gy to the right cerebellar lesion, and 22 Gy to the left parietal lesion. She tolerated the procedure without acute complications. In the two weeks following SRS, she had grade 1 fatigue and transient worsening of headache, managed with a dexamethasone taper. She completed the steroid taper without neurological deterioration, and her word-finding difficulties have improved substantially.

Following SRS, she underwent CT-guided core needle re-biopsy of the right upper lobe mass (along with sampling of the right paratracheal lymph node) to characterize resistance mechanisms. Pathology from that re-biopsy confirmed invasive adenocarcinoma with TTF-1 positivity. Rapid PCR confirmed EGFR L858R; T790M was not detected at or above the PCR detection threshold (1%). Full tissue NGS (FoundationOne CDx) was sent from that re-biopsy specimen and results are now available today.

Today she presents specifically to review the NGS re-profiling results and to formulate a systemic therapy plan. She reports that headaches have largely resolved following SRS, and her word-finding is back to near-normal. She denies new headaches, visual changes, focal weakness, seizures, or ataxia. She has mild fatigue, which she attributes to the recent procedures and the emotional burden of disease progression. Appetite is somewhat decreased but stable. She has not lost significant weight since the last visit. She is not on any systemic cancer therapy at this time.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Neurological: Headaches markedly improved since SRS; essentially resolved. Word-finding difficulty resolved. Denies seizures, diplopia, focal motor or sensory deficits, dizziness, or gait instability.
Pulmonary: Occasional dry cough, mild and intermittent. No hemoptysis. No dyspnea at rest; mild exertional dyspnea with brisk walking.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity edema at this time.
Gastrointestinal: Mild decreased appetite. No nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or abdominal pain. Bowel habits at baseline.
Musculoskeletal: Mild generalized fatigue. No bone pain or arthralgias.
Skin: Grade 1 acneiform rash (residual from prior Tagrisso) largely resolved since Tagrisso discontinuation approximately 8 weeks ago.
Constitutional: Fatigue, as noted. 2-pound weight loss since last visit (approximately 6 weeks ago). Denies fevers, chills, or night sweats.
Endocrine/Metabolic: No adrenal insufficiency symptoms following dexamethasone taper completion.
Psychosocial: Patient expresses anxiety regarding progression and the need for new therapy. Engaged and motivated; interested in clinical trial options.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vital Signs: Blood pressure 122/74 mmHg, heart rate 72 bpm (regular), respiratory rate 16 breaths/min, oxygen saturation 96% on room air, temperature 36.8 degrees Celsius, weight 56.2 kg (down approximately 1 kg from last visit).
General: Alert and oriented x3. Well-groomed, cooperative. Mild cushingoid features resolving after dexamethasone taper (mild facial fullness). ECOG performance status 1.
HEENT: Normocephalic. Pupils equal and reactive. Extraocular movements intact. No papilledema on fundoscopic exam. No lymphadenopathy palpable in cervical, supraclavicular, or axillary regions. Previously palpable right supraclavicular node is no longer appreciated.
Chest/Pulmonary: Mild decreased breath sounds at the right apex. No crackles, wheezes, or pleural rub. Chest wall is non-tender to palpation. No intercostal retractions.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No JVD. Peripheral pulses intact bilaterally.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly. No palpable masses.
Extremities: No peripheral edema at present. No calf tenderness. Skin over lower extremities intact.
Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Strength 5/5 in upper and lower extremities bilaterally. Sensation grossly intact. Finger-nose testing intact bilaterally. Gait steady, tandem gait performed without ataxia. Speech fluent; no word-finding pauses on conversational testing today (improved from prior visit). No dysarthria. Romberg negative.
Skin: Resolving scattered facial papules, largely cleared since Tagrisso discontinuation. No paronychia at present.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (obtained today)
Complete Blood Count: WBC 6.2 x 10^9/L, Hgb 11.4 g/dL (mild anemia, stable), Platelets 218 x 10^9/L, ANC 3.8 x 10^9/L.
Comprehensive Metabolic Panel: Sodium 138 mEq/L, Potassium 4.0 mEq/L, Creatinine 0.78 mg/dL (eGFR >60 mL/min/1.73m2), BUN 14 mg/dL, AST 22 U/L, ALT 28 U/L, Alkaline phosphatase 64 U/L, Total bilirubin 0.5 mg/dL, Albumin 3.6 g/dL, Glucose 98 mg/dL.
LDH: 196 U/L (upper limit of normal at this institution 210 U/L).
Magnesium: 1.9 mEq/L.

---

IMAGING RESULTS (reviewed from prior scans)
MRI brain (approximately 6 weeks ago, at progression): Three enhancing lesions as described above (left frontal 12 mm, right cerebellar 8 mm, left parietal 6 mm) with vasogenic edema around the left frontal lesion. No leptomeningeal enhancement. This imaging predates today's visit and guided the SRS treatment.
CT chest (at progression, approximately 8 weeks ago): Right upper lobe mass measured 2.7 cm (up from 1.8 cm on annual restaging); new right paratracheal node 1.1 cm. No hepatic or adrenal lesions at that time.
Post-SRS MRI brain: Not yet obtained; next brain MRI is scheduled for approximately 6 weeks post-SRS to assess treatment response.

---

PATHOLOGY / MOLECULAR RESULTS (re-biopsy, reviewed in detail today)
Tissue pathology (right upper lobe mass re-biopsy and right paratracheal lymph node): Invasive lung adenocarcinoma confirmed, morphologically similar to original biopsy specimen; TTF-1 positive, Napsin-A positive. Right paratracheal lymph node shows metastatic adenocarcinoma consistent with lung primary.

FoundationOne CDx re-profiling (tissue, right upper lobe mass):
- Tumor mutational burden: 6 mut/Mb (low-intermediate; increased from 4 mut/Mb at diagnosis)
- Microsatellite status: MSS
- EGFR L858R (exon 21): Confirmed, allele frequency 31.4%
- EGFR T790M: Detected at low allele frequency (3.1%) -- below standard high-level resistance threshold but above background; consistent with low-level sub-clonal T790M
- EGFR C797S: Not detected
- PIK3CA p.E545K (exon 9, pathogenic): Newly acquired; allele frequency 18.2% -- not present on initial NGS
- PTEN p.R130Q (exon 5, pathogenic): Newly acquired -- not present on initial NGS
- TP53 p.R248W: Persistent, allele frequency 35.1%
- SMARCA4 p.R1192H: Persistent (variant of uncertain significance)
- MET amplification: High-level (copy number ratio 5.1; FISH confirmatory testing pending at time of report)
- CCND1 gain: Persistent
- No new fusions detected (ALK, ROS1, RET, NTRK1/2/3 all negative)
- No ERBB2 amplification or mutation

Interpretation: This NGS profile is consistent with polyclonal, heterogeneous acquired resistance to Tagrisso. The dominant likely resistance mechanism is high-level MET amplification (copy number ratio 5.1), which is a well-established bypass signaling pathway enabling EGFR TKI resistance. Co-occurring newly acquired PIK3CA E545K and PTEN loss-of-function mutations suggest activation of the PI3K/AKT pathway as an additional resistance mechanism, which may also confer some degree of cross-resistance. Low-level T790M (3.1% AF on tissue; 0.6% on liquid biopsy) is present but at a level unlikely to be the dominant driver, especially in the context of Tagrisso therapy (which covers T790M). This represents a therapeutically complex scenario.

Case reviewed at molecular tumor board prior to today's visit. The consensus from the molecular tumor board is that high-level MET amplification is the dominant resistance driver and represents the primary therapeutic target. The PIK3CA/PTEN axis is a secondary concern and currently no approved targeted agent is available that specifically addresses this in combination with EGFR inhibition for this context.

---

ASSESSMENT
1. Stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma (right upper lobe primary, M1b with intracranial metastases), with acquired resistance to first-line osi at 14 months. Disease progression confirmed systemically (right upper lobe mass and mediastinal nodal recurrence) and intracranially (three brain metastases -- all treated with SRS approximately 6 weeks ago).

2. CNS disease: Post-SRS, patient is clinically improved. Headaches resolved, word-finding normalized. No new neurological concerns today. Post-SRS response imaging is scheduled.

3. Resistance mechanism profile (tissue NGS): Complex/polyclonal resistance with high-level MET amplification as primary driver, low-level T790M, and newly acquired PIK3CA E545K / PTEN R130Q as secondary alterations.

4. Systemic therapy: Currently off systemic therapy since osi discontinuation approximately 8 weeks ago (no therapy in interim while awaiting SRS and re-biopsy results). Patient maintains ECOG 1 performance status. Lab values within acceptable range for treatment initiation.

5. Mild normocytic anemia (Hgb 11.4) -- likely disease-related or steroid-related post-taper; not transfusion-dependent. Will recheck at next visit.

6. Psychosocial: Patient is anxious but engaged, motivated for continued treatment including clinical trial enrollment.

---

PLAN

1. Second-Line Systemic Therapy -- Bridging Chemotherapy:
Given the complexity of her resistance landscape and the absence of a currently available approved targeted regimen for MET-amplified EGFR-mutant NSCLC post-Tagrisso progression, we will initiate platinum-doublet chemotherapy as bridging systemic therapy. Plan is to proceed with Paraplatin (AUC 5 IV day 1) plus Alimta (500 mg/m2 IV day 1) every 21 days. PD-L1 TPS was only 5% on original biopsy; with the immunosuppressive context of concurrent steroids (recently completed) and low PD-L1, addition of immunotherapy at this time is not favored. Vitamin B12 and folic acid supplementation per standard pem protocol. Dexamethasone pre-medication for Alimta antifolate prophylaxis. Zofran PRN for nausea.

2. Clinical Trial Enrollment -- Primary Goal:
Active inquiry is underway for enrollment on a Phase II investigator-sponsored trial of savolitinib (a highly selective MET inhibitor) in combination with osi rechallenge for patients with MET-amplified, EGFR-mutant NSCLC following progression on third-generation EGFR TKI. The scientific rationale is well-supported: savolitinib would address the MET bypass pathway while Tagrisso rechallenge continues to suppress EGFR-dependent signaling and provides CNS coverage. Patient has been pre-screened and appears to meet eligibility criteria pending confirmatory FISH for MET amplification and final screening laboratories. carbo/Alimta will serve as bridging therapy while trial logistics are finalized; plan is to complete a limited number of chemotherapy cycles (approximately 3-4) before transitioning to the trial if she remains eligible. Trial coordinator has been notified. Informed consent process for the trial will be initiated at next visit.

3. CNS Monitoring:
Post-SRS brain MRI scheduled in approximately 2-4 weeks. Given the favorable CNS penetration profile of osi (to be restarted if clinical trial is initiated), we expect ongoing CNS disease control. Dexamethasone has been successfully tapered. No plans to restart steroids unless neurological symptoms recur.

4. Supportive Care:
- Anti-emetic protocol with ondansetron for chemotherapy days
- Proton pump inhibitor for GI protection during anticipated pem/dexamethasone administration
- Monitor CBC with differential on day 14 of each cycle given anticipated nadir
- If grade 2 or greater neutropenia, will consider adding G-CSF prophylaxis
- Nutritional counseling referral placed given mild weight loss and decreased appetite
- Social work referral offered and patient accepted, for psychosocial support and clinical trial navigation assistance

5. Laboratory / Biomarker Monitoring:
- MET amplification FISH confirmatory result pending; will follow up
- Repeat ctDNA (Guardant360) at cycle 2 to monitor for early molecular response and clonal dynamics on chemotherapy
- CBC, CMP, and LFTs at each cycle visit

6. Return to Clinic:
Patient will return in 3 weeks for cycle 1, day 1 of Paraplatin/Alimta. Lab results will be reviewed at that visit. Trial consent will be initiated.

Extensive time was spent with the patient and her husband today reviewing the NGS results in detail, explaining the concept of acquired resistance and the rationale for the proposed treatment sequence. She expressed understanding and agreement with the plan. She is particularly motivated by the prospect of clinical trial participation. The complexity of her resistance profile was explained in accessible language. All questions were answered.

Cancer is present at this assessment. Patient is systemic-therapy naive at this time (off osi for approximately 8 weeks); CNS disease is under post-SRS local treatment.

---
Dictated and reviewed by: Attending Thoracic Oncologist
Encounter type: Office visit, established patient, high-complexity medical decision-making
Time spent: Approximately 60 minutes, greater than 50% counseling and coordination of care
